decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective immediate action is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is the primary lever for lateral heat spreading and has room to grow within bounds.

changes.reason: Maximizing the spreader conductivity within the allowed bound (500.0) enhances lateral heat diffusion away from the chip, directly reducing the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower chip peak temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the upper bound
If this single change is insufficient, geometric adjustments (spreader width/height) will be required in the next step
